lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi there.

lines.speaker: Councillor Andrews
lines.text: Good day to you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who are you?

lines.speaker: Councillor Andrews
lines.text: My name is David Andrews. I'm the Head Councillor of the Kourend Council.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about the Kourend Council.

lines.speaker: Councillor Andrews
lines.text: Kourend's last King, Kharedst IV, had only one child when he died. She was too young to take the throne, so the Kourend Council was formed to look after things until she came of age.

lines.speaker: Councillor Andrews
lines.text: Alas, she was very close to her father and his sudden death drove her to insanity. With no suitable ruler, the Kourend Council was made permanent.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How is the Council structured?

lines.speaker: Councillor Andrews
lines.text: The Kourend Council is made up of four councillors. Every four years there is an election for the people to decide who the councillors should be.

lines.speaker: Councillor Andrews
lines.text: One of the councillors holds the position of Head Councillor and this rotates every year. I am the current Head Councillor.

lines.speaker: Councillor Andrews
lines.text: The other councillors are Councillor Hughes, Councillor Unkar and Councillor Orson.

lines.speaker: Councillor Andrews
lines.text: The other councillors are Councillor Unkar and Councillor Orson. The fourth seat on the Council is currently vacent after the recent arrest of Councillor Hughes.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So where do the five houses fit in?

lines.speaker: Councillor Andrews
lines.text: The five houses of Kourend each have their own leader who rules over their city. The leaders of the individual houses must enforce and obey the laws set out by the Kourend Council, but can otherwise rule as they see fit.

lines.speaker: Councillor Andrews
lines.text: I hope that helps.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, thank you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Time for me to go.

lines.speaker: Councillor Andrews
lines.text: Good day citizen.

lines.speaker: David Andrews
lines.text: Come to gloat?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well I do enjoy it when people get what they deserve.

lines.speaker: David Andrews
lines.text: You'll never know what it's like to be on top of the world. The power I had was unconquerable. I'd do it all again without hesitation.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You never had any power. You were just a puppet. You've always been nothing, and that's all you'll ever be.

lines.speaker: David Andrews
lines.text: That's not true!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Enjoy your cell, puppet. Won't be seeing you.
